2604.03135.title: AI-Assisted Unit Test Writing and Test-Driven Code Refactoring: A Case Study
2604.03135.paper_summary: Many software systems originate as prototypes or minimum viable products (MVPs), developed with an emphasis on delivery speed and responsiveness to changing requirements rather than long-term code maintainability. While effective for rapid delivery, this approach can result in codebases that are difficult to modify, presenting a significant opportunity cost in the era of AI-assisted or even AI-led programming. In this paper, we present a case study of using coding models for automated unit test generation and subsequent safe refactoring, with proposed code changes validated by passing tests. The study examines best practices for iteratively generating tests to capture existing system behavior, followed by model-assisted refactoring under developer supervision. We describe how this workflow constrained refactoring changes, the errors and limitations observed in both phases, the efficiency gains achieved, when manual intervention was necessary, and how we addressed the weak value misalignment we observed in models. Using this approach, we generated nearly 16,000 lines of reliable unit tests in hours rather than weeks, achieved up to 78\% branch coverage in critical modules, and significantly reduced regression risk during large-scale refactoring. These results illustrate software engineering's shift toward an empirical science, emphasizing data collection and constraining mechanisms that support fast, safe iteration.
2604.03135.paper_summary_zh: 許多軟體系統起初是作為原型或最小可行產品（MVP）而開發的，重點在於交付速度和對變更需求的響應，而不是長期的程式碼可維護性。雖然這種方法對於快速交付是有效的，但可能導致難以修改的程式碼庫，在人工智慧輔助甚至人工智慧主導的程式設計時代，這造成了顯著的機會成本。在本文中，我們提出了一個使用編碼模型進行自動單元測試生成和隨後安全重構的案例研究，所提議的程式碼變更通過測試驗證。該研究考察了迭代生成測試以捕捉現有系統行為的最佳實踐，然後在開發者監督下進行模型輔助的重構。我們描述了這一工作流程如何限制重構變更、在兩個階段觀察到的錯誤和限制、所實現的效率增益、何時需要手動介入，以及我們如何解決在模型中觀察到的弱價值不對齊。使用這種方法，我們在幾小時內生成了近16,000行可靠的單元測試，而不是幾週，並在關鍵模組中實現了高達78\%的分支覆蓋率，並在大規模重構期間顯著降低了回歸風險。這些結果展示了軟體工程向實證科學的轉變，強調數據收集和支持快速、安全迭代的約束機制。
2604.03135.author: Ema Smolic et.al.
2604.03135.authors: Ema Smolic, Mario Brcic, Luka Hobor, Mihael Kovac
